title: System and method of local retransmission of tcp/ip frames
abstract: A packet is received from a first link, and includes identification of a sender and a destination. An acknowledgement of receiving the packet is transmitted toward the sender, and the packet is forwarded, over a second link, toward the destination. A copy of the packet is stored in a queue. Upon detecting that the destination failed to receive the packet forwarded over the second link, the copy of the packet is retrieved from the queue and re-transmitted over the second link to the destination.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method for reducing sender retransmission in a packet communication network, comprising: receiving a packet from a first link, the packet identifying a sender and a destination; transmitting toward the sender an acknowledgement of receiving the packet; forwarding the packet, over a second link, toward the destination; based at least in part on receiving the packet from the first link, storing a copy of the packet in a queue, as a queued copy of the packet; and detecting an event indicative of the destination failing to receive the packet forwarded over the second link and, in response, retransmitting the packet, based on the queued copy of the packet. 2. The method of claim 1, further comprising: detecting an event indicative of the destination receiving the packet forwarded over second link and, in response, deleting the queued copy of the packet. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein retransmitting the packet, based on the queued copy of the packet, includes retrieving the queued copy of the packet from the local queue, and transmitting the queued copy to the client. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein retransmitting the packet, based on the queued copy of the packet, is over the second link. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein the packet is Transmission Control Protocol / Internet Protocol (TCP/IP) packet, and wherein the method further comprises: establishing a TCP/IP connection between the sender and the destination; and maintaining the TCP/IP connection during the transmitting. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the transmitting toward the sender the acknowledgement of receiving the packet configures the acknowledgement to appear to the sender as if sent by the destination in accordance with the TCP/IP connection. 7. The method of claim 5, further comprising: after establishing the TCP/IP connection between the sender and the destination, transmitting the packet from the sender into a network connected to the first link, then from the network to the first link. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein the first link includes a satellite gateway server, an uplink from the satellite gateway server to a satellite, and a downlink from the satellite to a terminal within a spot beam of the satellite. 9. The method of claim 1, further comprising: monitoring a fill state of the queue and, based on a result of the monitoring, switching a flag between a value indicating one among a filled state and a not-filled state and a value indicating the other among the filled state and the not-filled state. 10. The method of claim 9, wherein storing a copy of the packet in the queue is further based, at least in part, on the flag indicating the not filled state. 11. The method of claim 10, further comprising: based, at least in part, on the flag indicating the filled state, communicating back to the sender a request to throttle down transmission of packets. 12. The method of claim 1, wherein the event indicative of the destination failing to receive the packet forwarded over second link includes a message indicating failure. 13. The method of claim 11, wherein the packet is a Transmission Control Protocol / Internet Protocol (TCP/IP) packet, and wherein the message indicating failure is a TCP/IP selective acknowledgement (SACK) message. 14. An apparatus for reducing sender retransmission in a packet communication network, comprising: a local queue; an interface to a first network, configured to receive a packet from the first network, identifying a sender and identifying a destination, and to transmit into the first network, toward the sender, an acknowledgement of having received the packet, wherein the interface to the first network is configured to store a copy of the packet in the local queue, based at least in part on the interface to the first network receiving the packet; and a local transmit and retransmit logic, configured to receive the packet from the interface to the first network, transmit the packet into a second network, toward the destination, and receive an indicator from the second network that the destination did not receive the packet and, in response, to retrieve the copy of the packet from the local queue and retransmit the copy toward the destination. 15. The apparatus of claim 14, wherein the local transmit and retransmit logic is further configured to receive from the second network an indication that the destination received the packet and, in response, to delete the copy of the packet from the local queue. 16. The apparatus of claim 14, further comprising a local queue manager configured to monitor a fill state of the local queue, and based on the monitoring to set a fill flag and wherein the the interface to the first network is further configured to store a copy of the packet in the local queue, based at least in part on a combination of the interface to the first network receiving the packet and the fill flag indicating the local queue is not full. 17. The apparatus of claim 14, wherein the local transmit and retransmit logic is configured to retransmit the copy over the second link. 18. The apparatus of claim 14, wherein the packet is a Transmission Control Protocol / Internet Protocol (TCP/IP) packet. 19. The apparatus of claim 18, wherein the interface to the first network is configured to transmit the acknowledgement to appear, at the sender, as if sent by the destination as a communication within a TCP/I P connection between the sender and the destination. 20. The apparatus of claim 19, wherein the acknowledgment is a TCP/IP selective acknowledgement (SACK) message.
